patch.value: Capability: CLI command surface & agent ergonomics
Evolve the CLI command surface and lifecycle primitives for agent and human ergonomics (bulk ops, query filters, hierarchy views, history diffs)
Evolve the CLI command surface and lifecycle primitives for agent and human ergonomics (bulk ops, query filters, hierarchy views, history diffs)
Faster, safer agent/human workflows over the core item lifecycle
Never-block error contract holds across the command surface (recovery bundle with next steps on every failure path); agent-UX audit findings closed or waived.

## Charter
Living capability epic for the CLI command surface and agent ergonomics: lifecycle primitives (create/update/close/claim/next), bulk operations, query filters, hierarchy views, output projections, and never-block error UX. pm is agent-first: every command must be usable by an agent with zero out-of-band context, and every error must carry a machine-actionable recovery path.

## Scope
- In: command surface additions/refinements, flag ergonomics, output formats (toon/json/csv/table), never-block guidance, did-you-mean suggestions, agent token-efficiency of outputs.
- Out: MCP tool surface (pm-5k4v), storage/history internals (pm-o2kc), extension-owned commands (pm-ugqx).

## Status
97 closed children delivered the v0.1 surface (next, focus, plan family, update-many, list projections, close structured fields, shortcuts, aliases). This epic stays OPEN as a living backlog per the capability-epic convention — new command-surface work lands here.

## Open children (2026-07-04)
- pm-qt5d — --pm-path package install leaks extensions into caller workspace (path-scoping safety).
- pm-gdi7 — unknown-option flag suggestions, list-closed projection, schema add-field help discoverability.
- pm-b3e9 — pre-install package-owned command names should hint the owning install command.

## Update 2026-07-05
New initiative epic parented here: **pm-n7rr — CLI surface simplification** (noun-verb consolidation, ~60 -> ~15 top-level commands, zero feature loss). Children: pm-a22j token baseline, pm-0z7n alias shim layer, pm-pfqi list-* consolidation, pm-tqel history namespace, pm-tnud package namespace, pm-eq4x lifecycle sugar fold, pm-o3fh calendar sugar to builtin-calendar, pm-6apl ops namespace, pm-e2bq tiered contracts-driven help, pm-1kxs agent story. Grammar ADR: pm-pbyu (under pm-m2u1).

Composability contract set filed here (stated product principle: pm composes with grep/jq/xargs for on-the-fly complex queries): pm-m9xv persona story, pm-646c NDJSON output mode for list/search/context, pm-psy1 test-gated scripting contract (exit codes, stdout/stderr discipline, field-name stability). Related existing items: pm-kipd stdin JSON create/update, pm-cfed lean JSON.

message: Bundle agent-UX token-efficiency implementation across list/contracts surfaces

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Verification evidence: full coverage passed at 100/100/100/100 (5462 tests), lint/static/docs/contracts gates passed, and packed-install temp proof passed in /tmp/pm-agent-ux-proof-TYSgf1 for pm list --today, pm list --recent, pm contracts --summary, and SDK runAction({action:'contracts', options:{summary:true}}).

author: claude-code-agent
message: Release claim after documenting current bundle progress; not closing unresolved scope.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Evidence update: CI Gates (static) failed because the committed contract snapshot was generated before rebuilding dist after tool-parameter metadata copy changes. Rebuilt with pnpm build, regenerated with pnpm contracts:update, and reran the full static CI sequence locally: pnpm version:check && pnpm changelog:pm:check && pnpm contracts:check && pnpm security:scan && pnpm lint && npm pack --dry-run. Result: passed.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence: addressed latest bot feedback before repush. Fixed SDK/MCP search contract leakage by keeping list-only --today/--recent out of search schemas, added help to contracts summary intents, treated programmatic updatedAfter:null as unselected, and clarified open-only today docs. Verification passed: focused tests (78 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5463 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed the final Gemini normalizer finding by making normalizeExtensionFlagName accept unknown, ignore null/blank values, trim valid strings, and reject non-string values with a deterministic TypeError. Added focused coverage. Verification passed: focused tests (79 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5464 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed final Gemini edge cases by ignoring blank updatedAfter values in list window conflict detection and adding more core command intents (guide, ops, reindex, focus, telemetry) to contracts summary output. Verification passed: focused tests (79 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5464 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed CodeRabbit schema feedback by expanding the shared summary parameter description to include health rollups and adding contracts action schema mutual-exclusion rules for projection flags. Verification passed: focused tests (80 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5465 tests).

value.note: README badge metadata touched during final verification bundle

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed Greptile pushed-review schema/runtime mismatch by adding action-schema mutual-exclusion rules for list today/recent/updatedAfter windows across list variants. Verification passed: focused tests (81 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5466 tests).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review evidence update: addressed final value-sensitive schema feedback by making schema mutual exclusion conditional on selected boolean values, adding completion summary intent, and rejecting bare '-' short extension flags. Verification passed: focused tests (81 tests), CI-equivalent static sequence, and full coverage 100/100/100/100 (5466 tests).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Agent/developer-experience program map (2026-07-11, maintainer directive: drastically improve pm CLI DX; documentation-only pass). This epic owns CLI-side DX; this note is the ledger — extend it on future DX asks, never re-brainstorm.

DX FOR THIS TOOL = what an agent pays per task: tokens read, retries burned on flag/shape traps, commands memorized. Three existing lanes already carry most of it:
1. GRAMMAR (epic pm-n7rr + ADR pm-pbyu + shim pm-0z7n + baseline pm-a22j): 65 top-level commands -> ~12 noun clusters, shared verb vocabulary, one-screen help target. The census/sequencing notes on pm-n7rr are the plan of record.
2. TOKEN SHAPE (this epic's children): pm-cfed lean JSON (+48-53% vs TOON today), pm-g9xk lean errors, pm-ishm repeated-tags bloat, pm-nilh 12.7x mutation echo; pm-vxxm contracts --summary SHIPPED as the agent bootstrap path.
3. TRAP ELIMINATION: NEW pm-srns (filed today) = uniform format-flag grammar + single envelope spec across every command; pm-gdi7 unknown-option suggestions; pm-p3x4 .activity decoy; pm-a9mc validate counts-only.

SHIPPED DX wins (fence — never re-file): PR#502 caller-aware next/held_by_others + claim --next; pm-b84u repeated --ac accumulates; pm-zazb dep-shorthand fail-fast with hint; pm-2ldo hybrid search quoting; pm-1h7w priority range hint; pm-fy8o invalid-type hint; comments --body/--comment aliases.

CANDIDATE LEVERS (gated on pm-a22j token baseline numbers; promote to items only with evidence):
a. Golden agent-transcript replay — measure tokens-per-completed-task on recorded agent sessions, not per-command; makes DX regressions CI-visible (extends pm-cu1i gate, never a new harness).
b. Machine-readable deprecation channel — when the 0z7n shim rewrites an alias, envelope carries the canonical spelling so agents self-migrate (pm-0mox counters are the read side).
c. Exit-code contract — uniform semantic exit codes documented in the envelope spec (pm-srns's spec is the home; today create/update failures hide under pipes).
d. First-run agent orientation — `pm contracts --summary` + `pm next` as the documented two-call cold-start; belongs in docs + plugin templates, not new commands.
REJECTED (constraints, not oversights): no new top-level commands for DX features (grammar rule: flags on existing nouns, pm-plwm precedent); no interactive/TUI affordances on the agent path; no output redesign outside the pm-srns/pm-cfed envelope spec (one spec, not per-command taste).
